annotations.title: What is Ownership? - The Rust Programming Language
Understanding Ownership - The Rust Programming Language
A Grounded Conceptual Model for Ownership Types in Rust

content.text:
Understanding Rust's ownership model is crucial for effective memory management and ensuring program safety without a garbage collector. Here are some authoritative and recent resources that provide comprehensive explanations:

1. **The Rust Programming Language Book**: This official resource offers an in-depth chapter on ownership, detailing its rules and implications. ([doc.rust-lang.org](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch04-01-what-is-ownership.html?highlight=garbage%2Ccollector&utm_source=openai))

2. **Rust Documentation**: The official Rust documentation provides a detailed explanation of ownership, including its rules and how it ensures memory safety. ([doc.rust-lang.org](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch04-00-understanding-ownership.html?utm_source=openai))

3. **Rust (programming language) - Wikipedia**: This Wikipedia article offers a comprehensive overview of Rust's ownership system, including its rules and how it ensures memory safety. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rust_%28programming_language%29?utm_source=openai))

4. **Rust Syntax - Wikipedia**: This article provides an overview of Rust's syntax, including its ownership and reference system. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rust_syntax?utm_source=openai))

5. **A Grounded Conceptual Model for Ownership Types in Rust**: This academic paper presents a conceptual model for understanding ownership types in Rust, aiming to improve the pedagogy of ownership. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.04134?utm_source=openai))

6. **Ownership in Rust Explained - YouTube**: This video provides a simple and comprehensive explanation of Rust's ownership system, including stack and heap allocation.

These resources should provide a solid foundation for understanding Rust's ownership model and its role in memory management.
